### Post by 449 on 2008-01-18
I think this is because I used Fluxkeys... How to I make it so I can click again?

```
# File generated by FluxConf
None Mouse1top :HideMenus
None Mouse2top :WorkspaceMenu
None Mouse3top :RootMenu
None Mouse4top :NextWorkspace
None Mouse5top :PrevWorkspace
Mod1 Tab :NextWindow
Mod1 Shift Tab :PrevWindow
Control Shift F1 :Quit
Mod1 F2 :Workspace 2
Mod1 F3 :Workspace 3
Mod1 F4 :Workspace 4
Mod1 F5 :Workspace 5
Mod1 F6 :Workspace 6
Mod1 F7 :Workspace 7
Mod1 F8 :Workspace 8
Mod1 F9 :Workspace 9
Mod1 F10 :Workspace 10
Mod1 F11 :Workspace 11
Mod1 F12 :Workspace 12
```

Thanks!

### Post by 449 on 2008-01-18
Can anyone help me out with this?

---

### Post by Patb on 2008-03-19
449.  Fluxkeys seems to suffer from a major bug.  I edit my key bindings at ~/.fluxbox/keys manually.  

Find the original default keys file which is at /etc/X11/fluxbox/keys or /usr/share/fluxbox/keys or /usr/local/share/fluxbox/keys.  You can copy this to replace ~/.fluxbox/keys or you can edit the latter file to include the mouse key bindings from the default.  

For more details, see [this thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=617812").  (Or even do a search "fluxkeys mouse" ;))

Cheers, Pat.

PS: If this solves the problem, use the "Thread Tools" button to mark it "Solved".
